problems.title: Extend List
problems.description: Extend the list by appending elements from the iterable. Expected output: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Extend the list by appending elements from the iterable. Expected output: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
problems.starter_code:
def main(nums, to_add):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    nums = [1, 2, 3]

    to_add = [4, 5, 6]
    main(nums, to_add)
problems.solution:
def main(nums, to_add):
    nums.extend(to_add)
    print(nums)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    nums = [1, 2, 3]

    to_add = [4, 5, 6]
    main(nums, to_add)

problems.title: Zip Two Lists
problems.description: Combine two lists element-wise. Expected output: [(1, 'a'), (2, 'b'), (3, 'c')]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Combine two lists element-wise. Expected output: [(1, 'a'), (2, 'b'), (3, 'c')]
problems.starter_code:
def main(nums, chars):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    nums = [1, 2, 3]

    chars = ['a', 'b', 'c']
    main(nums, chars)
problems.solution:
def main(nums, chars):
    zipped = list(zip(nums, chars))
    print(zipped)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    nums = [1, 2, 3]

    chars = ['a', 'b', 'c']
    main(nums, chars)
